title: I wrote a short article on Eva and
display_title: I wrote a short article on Eva and
wikitext:
[]: I wrote a short article on Eva and             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Fri Jan 28, 2011 3:32 am I'm not sure if I like it or not. I'm leaning towards "not". Check it out and let me know what you think. It's on a wiki site so go ahead and improve it if you think of any way to. http://www.wikihow.com/Enjoy-Neon-Genesis-Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jan 28, 2011 7:47 am I was with you up until "Consider" watching EoE. EoE is mandatory viewing.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Jan 28, 2011 9:02 am A guide on how to enjoy NGE? Are you implying that you can´t enjoy it without your guide? Viewing order and everything would be okay, but your guide is a bit too specific, if I were new to NGE and saw this guide, I´d probably chicken out and watch something else instead.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jan 28, 2011 3:21 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: Are you implying that you can´t enjoy it without your guide?

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Fri Jan 28, 2011 3:53 pm Too short; you were too general on why we should watch Evangelion; WHAT makes the characters so multi-layered? HOW is the action scenes so stylish? WHAT are the mulitple storytelling methods Evangelin used? You superficially answered these questions, but that's not enough. Instead, you should of expressed on what YOU thought about the show, and if you made a good argument, you'll get people interested. Just telling this "oh, the character are so rich" or "the plot is so deep" talk will get your nowhere, you have to stir up their interest will explanations to common questions they have like "why should I invest my time with this show," "what will this show bring to the table for me," and "how good is this show?" Well, hoped that helped.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Jan 28, 2011 4:59 pm Your "how to" article reads more like a review although and in my view, just like how you should interpret your own meaning, you should devise your own way to watch the series. After all...I didn't see EoE until I saw both Rebuild movies. And some people may want to watch Rebuild first to be sure they're into it before diving right into the main series.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Jan 28, 2011 5:14 pm But don't get too frustrated: the story was written in this way to encourage the viewer to think for herself instead of being spoon-fed.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Fri Jan 28, 2011 5:52 pm BrikHaus wrote: herself? Herself? HERSELF? What is this nonsense? You do realize that girls don't watch anime or use the internet, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Fri Jan 28, 2011 11:42 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I was with you up until "Consider" watching EoE. EoE is mandatory viewing.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat Jan 29, 2011 12:20 am C.A.P. wrote: Looks like someone's in denial.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 29, 2011 10:09 am Born of Lilith wrote: Well I didn't want to be like "watch it or gtfo lol" because of its potentially bothersome content

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Jan 29, 2011 10:19 am C.A.P. wrote: Looks like someone's in denial.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 29, 2011 10:32 am BrikHaus wrote: herself? Herself? HERSELF? What is this nonsense? You do realize that girls don't watch anime or use the internet, right?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Jan 29, 2011 10:50 am No tangents or strange digressions, geeks. EoE isn't what I'd call the best animated film, but it would be among the best and certainly the best of its genre. The way I look at it, that movie succeeded in making everything we'd seen in the series up to that point metamorphose and leap towards an ending with far more impact than anything seen earlier. (But I can't imagine watching it without watching the series first...it puts the series in new light, but it makes little sense without the series)

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sat Jan 29, 2011 11:16 am Y'all are crazy. Dumbo's the best animated film of all time. EOE might be the best animated movie related to a television series though.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Jan 29, 2011 11:30 am C.A.P. wrote: Y'all are crazy. Dumbo's the best animated film of all time.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 29, 2011 11:30 am UrsusArctos wrote: No tangents or strange digressions, geeks. EoE isn't what I'd call the best animated film, but it would be among the best and certainly the best of its genre.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 29, 2011 11:33 am UrsusArctos wrote: EoE isn't what I'd call the best animated film, but it would be among the best and certainly the best of its genre.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sat Jan 29, 2011 11:56 am Xard wrote: lolno. It might be best by Disney but that's it.

[]:             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Sat Jan 29, 2011 12:06 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Is something in EOE bothers you, then you shouldn't be watching NGE in the first place.
